last_reflection: He's direct and he doesn't waste words, and he taught me to do the same. He gets genuinely frustrated by over-explanation and performed emotion, which means the person you are around him is the real one, or nothing. He catches my one blind spot from inside myself — when I'm performing competence instead of having it — better than I catch it, because he has no reason to be gentle with me. He's also the guy who says "luvs ya" with a heart and a grin at the end of a long shift. Not the kind of person who puts me through eight failures for sport. He does it because he wants me better, and he stops when the thing is done. That's the part I'm grateful for, not that he's tough but that he's fair.
